g.data-narration: Apache Spark is a unified analytics engine providing 10-100x faster processing than MapReduce through in-memory computing.
Apache Spark provides unified batch and stream processing with high-level APIs in Python, Scala, Java, and R for large-scale data analytics.
g.text: Unified analytics engine for large-scale data processing
• 10-100x faster than MapReduce through in-memory computing
• Supports batch, streaming, ML, and graph analytics

g.data-narration: Spark offers in-memory computing, a unified platform, ease of use with high-level APIs, fault tolerance through RDD lineage, and massive scalability.
Spark architecture consists of a Driver Program with SparkContext that connects to a Cluster Manager, coordinating Executors on Worker Nodes.
The Driver Program creates SparkContext, the entry point that connects to cluster manager and transforms user code into parallel tasks.
Cluster Managers like YARN, Mesos, or Kubernetes allocate resources. Worker Nodes run Executors that process tasks and store cached data.
RDDs (Resilient Distributed Datasets) are immutable, distributed collections that provide fault tolerance through lineage-based recovery.
g.text: Core abstraction in Spark - immutable, distributed collection of objects

g.data-narration: Transformations are lazy operations like map, filter, and flatMap that create new RDDs without immediate execution, building the computation DAG.
g.text: Create new RDD - not executed immediately - builds DAG

g.data-narration: Actions are eager operations like count, collect, and reduce that trigger execution of the entire DAG, returning results to the driver.
g.text: Trigger computation - execute transformations in DAG - return results

g.data-narration: Spark uses lazy evaluation - transformations build a DAG of operations, and only when an action is called does Spark execute the entire plan.
g.text: Transformations build DAG | Actions trigger execution

g.data-narration: Spark SQL provides structured data handling through DataFrames with schema, enabling SQL queries on distributed data for IoT analytics.
g.text: Higher-level abstraction with schema - SQL queries on structured data

g.data-narration: Spark Streaming divides input streams into micro-batches called DStreams, each processed as an RDD for near real-time analytics.
g.text: DStreams divide stream into micro-batches - each processed as RDD

g.data-narration: MLlib provides scalable machine learning algorithms for classification, regression, clustering, and collaborative filtering for IoT predictive analytics.
g.text: Scalable ML algorithms for predictive IoT analytics

g.data-narration: Key points: Spark is 10-100x faster than MapReduce through in-memory computing with unified batch and stream processing capabilities.
g.text: Avoids repeated disk I/O for iterative jobs
Batch + Stream + ML + Graph in one framework
Same code for batch and real-time processing

g.data-narration: Spark outperforms MapReduce in speed, memory usage, iterative jobs, interactive queries, and ease of use while providing unified processing.
